# AI Aquarium — QA Test Suite

Playwright regression tests for [AI Aquarium](https://ai-aquarium-phaser.vercel.app).

Tests run against the live deployment and cover all M1 features.

---

## Setup

```bash
npm install
npx playwright install chromium
```

---

## Running Tests

### Full gate (smoke → regression → critical)

```bash
npx playwright test
```

### By tier

```bash
npm run test:smoke       # Fast smoke (8 tests, ~15s)
npm run test:regression  # Full regression suite
npm run test:critical    # Critical loop integrity
```

### Specific spec files

```bash
# M1 debug dashboard
npx playwright test tests/regression/m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js

# M1 activity duration + AI cycle
npx playwright test tests/regression/m1-activity-duration.spec.js

# Slot system / capacity
npx playwright test tests/regression/slot-system.spec.js

# Talk handshaking
npx playwright test tests/regression/talk-handshaking.spec.js

# Fallback logic
npx playwright test tests/regression/fallback-logic.spec.js
```

### With UI (for debugging)

```bash
npx playwright test --ui
```

---

## Configuration

| Setting | Default | Override |
|---------|---------|----------|
| `BASE_URL` | `https://ai-aquarium-phaser.vercel.app` | `.env` file or env var |
| Timeout | 60s per test | `playwright.config.js` |
| Retries | 1 | `playwright.config.js` |

To run against a local or staging deployment:

```bash
BASE_URL=http://localhost:3000 npx playwright test
```

---

## Test Structure

```
tests/
├── helpers.js                          # Shared helpers (waitForEngine, pollEngineState, waitForDashboard)
├── smoke/
│   └── app-loads.spec.js               # Page loads, engine init, 3 characters, 10 objects
├── regression/
│   ├── ai-decisions.spec.js            # AI LLM requests, valid action types, 30s interval, dialogue fields
│   ├── character-movement.spec.js      # A* pathfinding, tile movement, bounds check
│   ├── character-facing.spec.js        # FRG-244: facing toward objects + characters during talk
│   │                                   #   (runs against local dev server at localhost:5173)
│   ├── walk-animation.spec.js          # FRG-204: walk animation stutter/freeze fix
│   │                                   #   moveTo resets walk state, large-delta frame accumulation,
│   │                                   #   full 4-frame cycle, walkTimer remainder invariant
│   │                                   #   (runs against local dev server at localhost:5174)
│   ├── contextual-interaction-animations.spec.js  # FRG-243: contextual anim per object type,
│   │                                   #   spriteKey/interactAnim during+after interaction, live sim,
│   │                                   #   all 9 interaction sprites (runs against localhost:3000)
│   ├── object-interaction.spec.js      # Object state transitions, debug panel
│   ├── speech-bubbles.spec.js          # Dialogue lines, pendingSpeech, speech timer
│   ├── m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js      # Debug page layout, real-time updates, pause/resume,
│   │                                   #   position/facing/target fields, reset behavior, speed controls
│   ├── m1-activity-duration.spec.js    # Activity timers, 30s AI cycle, action types
│   ├── slot-system.spec.js             # Slot/capacity display, pip indicators, reservations,
│   │                                   #   multi-capacity simultaneous use (kotatsu/tv)
│   ├── talk-handshaking.spec.js        # TALK/REJECT events, talking state, canTalk filter
│   └── fallback-logic.spec.js          # FALLBACK events, plan changes, reservation release
└── critical/
    └── sim-loop-integrity.spec.js      # End-to-end: LLM → move → object change → no errors
```

---

## Feature Coverage Map

| M1 Feature | Spec File(s) |
|------------|-------------|
| Characters: Maple, Sol, Nyx | `smoke/app-loads.spec.js`, `m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js` |
| AI decisions every ~30s | `ai-decisions.spec.js`, `m1-activity-duration.spec.js` |
| Action types: move, interact, idle, talk | `ai-decisions.spec.js`, `m1-activity-duration.spec.js` |
| LLM response: dialogue + description fields | `ai-decisions.spec.js` |
| A* pathfinding, bounds | `character-movement.spec.js`, `m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js` |
| Object state machines (idle → active → idle) | `object-interaction.spec.js`, `m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js` |
| Activity hold timers | `m1-activity-duration.spec.js`, `m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js` |
| Smart objects slot system (capacity, pips) | `slot-system.spec.js` |
| Multi-capacity simultaneous use (kotatsu, tv) | `slot-system.spec.js` |
| Walk animation: moveTo reset, large-delta drain, 4-frame cycle | `walk-animation.spec.js` |
| Character facing toward objects on interact | `character-facing.spec.js` |
| Character facing toward each other on talk | `character-facing.spec.js` |
| Contextual interaction animations (reading/sit/phone) | `contextual-interaction-animations.spec.js` |
| Talk handshaking (TALK/REJECT events) | `talk-handshaking.spec.js` |
| Fallback logic (FALLBACK events) | `fallback-logic.spec.js` |
| Debug dashboard: layout + real-time | `m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js` |
| Debug dashboard: position, facing, target fields | `m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js` |
| Speed controls (functional 2x/4x verify) | `m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js` |
| Reset button (behavior, not just visibility) | `m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js` |
| Full sim loop integrity | `critical/sim-loop-integrity.spec.js` |

---

## CI (GitHub Actions)

Tests run automatically on every push and pull request via `.github/workflows/playwright.yml`.

Results are stored as artifacts (JSON report + test-results folder).

To run CI locally with the same config:

```bash
npm ci
npx playwright install --with-deps chromium
npx playwright test
```

---

## Known Issues

| Issue | File | Notes |
|-------|------|-------|
| `talk` action rarely observed in short runs | `talk-handshaking.spec.js` | AI is non-deterministic; tests gracefully skip if not observed |
| REJECT events are probabilistic | `talk-handshaking.spec.js` | Only fires when two characters attempt simultaneous talk |
| FALLBACK events require 30s+ observation | `fallback-logic.spec.js` | Events appear after AI cycle + object contention |
| Sim clock ratio ~36x in headless (vs 60x in browser) | `critical/sim-loop-integrity.spec.js` | RAF throttled in headless — documented, not a product bug |

---

## Test Results (last run: 2026-03-09, FRG-204 added)

| Suite | Tests | Status |
|-------|-------|--------|
| Smoke | 8 | ✅ 8/8 |
| Regression (ai-decisions) | 6 | ✅ 6/6 |
| Regression (character-movement) | 4 | ✅ 4/4 |
| Regression (character-facing) | 5 | ✅ 5/5 |
| Regression (walk-animation) | 4 | ✅ 4/4 |
| Regression (contextual-interaction-animations) | 5 | ✅ 5/5 |
| Regression (object-interaction) | 5 | ✅ 5/5 |
| Regression (speech-bubbles) | 4 | ✅ 4/4 |
| Regression (m1-debug-dashboard) | 24 | ✅ 24/24 |
| Regression (m1-activity-duration) | 10 | ✅ 10/10 |
| Regression (slot-system) | 11 | ✅ 11/11 |
| Regression (talk-handshaking) | 6 | ✅ 6/6 |
| Regression (fallback-logic) | 5 | ✅ 5/5 |
| Critical | 5 | ✅ 5/5 |
| **Total** | **102** | **✅ 102/102** |

<html lang="en">
<head>
  <meta charset="UTF-8" />
  <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0" />
  <title>AI Aquarium — Maple's Café</title>
  <style>
    * { margin: 0; padding: 0; box-sizing: border-box; }
    html, body { width: 100%; height: 100%; background: #1a1a2e; overflow: hidden; }
    canvas { image-rendering: pixelated; image-rendering: crisp-edges; display: block; }

    /* ── Debug toggle button ── */
    #debug-toggle {
      position: fixed;
      top: 10px;
      right: 10px;
      z-index: 100;
      padding: 4px 10px;
      background: #222240;
      color: #e0ddd4;
      border: 1px solid #444;
      border-radius: 3px;
      cursor: pointer;
      font-family: 'Courier New', Courier, monospace;
      font-size: 11px;
      opacity: 0.75;
      transition: opacity 0.15s;
    }
    #debug-toggle:hover { opacity: 1; background: #2a2a5a; }
    #debug-toggle.open  { opacity: 1; border-color: #6a9a8a; color: #9eebd4; background: #1e2e3e; }

+// window._gameEngine is set unconditionally in scene.js create() — no injection needed.
+// Characters identified by .name ('Amelia', 'Lucy', 'Ash') not .id (which doesn't exist in master).
+// Bug: startTalk() always played {name}_idle_anim (generic down-facing idle) for both partners,
+//      making characters appear to look away from each other during conversations.
+// Fix: Added _playDirectionalIdle() which plays {name}_idle_{facing}, and startTalk() calls it
+//      after _startConversation() sets the correct facing direction for each character.
+test.describe('FRG-244 (directional idle): conversations use directional idle animations', () => {
+  // ── TEST 6: _startConversation sets facing and plays directional idle ─────────
+  test('_startConversation plays {name}_idle_{facing} for both partners (horizontal)', async ({ page }) => {
+      // Position Amelia to the left of Lucy (dx = 3 > 0 → Amelia faces right, Lucy faces left)
+      // Reset facings to a wrong value so we can see the correction
+      // engine._startConversation: faces both toward each other, then calls startTalk on each
+    // Amelia (tx=6) is left of Lucy (tx=9) → Amelia faces right, Lucy faces left
+    expect(result.amelia.facing, 'Amelia should face right toward Lucy').toBe('right');
+    expect(result.lucy.facing,   'Lucy should face left toward Amelia').toBe('left');
+    // FRG-244 fix: animation key must be directional, NOT generic idle_anim
+      'Amelia must play Amelia_idle_right (not Amelia_idle_anim) when talking'
+      'Lucy must play Lucy_idle_left (not Lucy_idle_anim) when talking'
+    expect(result.amelia.action, 'Amelia should be in talking state').toBe('talking');
+    expect(result.lucy.action,   'Lucy should be in talking state').toBe('talking');
+  // ── TEST 7: vertical conversation — up/down directional idles ────────────────
+  test('_startConversation plays directional idle for vertical character arrangement', async ({ page }) => {
+      // Position Ash above Lucy (dx=0, dy=3 → Ash faces down, Lucy faces up)
+    // Ash (ty=4) above Lucy (ty=7) → Ash faces down, Lucy faces up
+    expect(result.ash.facing,  'Ash should face down toward Lucy').toBe('down');
+    expect(result.lucy.facing, 'Lucy should face up toward Ash').toBe('up');
+    // FRG-244 fix: directional idle, not generic idle_anim
+  // ── TEST 8: _playDirectionalIdle matches facing for all 4 directions ─────────

+// Runs against master dev server at localhost:5177 (same server as tests 1-5 above).
+// No injection needed; we fetch the PNG assets directly from the static server.
+// Bug: stove_on.png was a byte-for-byte copy of stove_off.png, so toggling the
+//      stove texture key had no visible effect — both states looked identical.
+// Fix: stove_on.png was replaced with a new lit-stove image (distinct pixel data).
+test.describe('FRG-246 (asset-diff): stove_on.png is visually distinct from stove_off.png', () => {
+  test('stove_off.png and stove_on.png have different byte lengths', async ({ page }) => {
+      'must have different file sizes — identical sizes indicate the same asset'
+  // ── TEST 7: byte content differs in PNG header / IDAT chunks ─────────────────
+  test('stove_off.png and stove_on.png have different byte content (not identical files)', async ({ page }) => {
+        return { error: `PNG fetch failed: off=${offResp.status} on=${onResp.status}` };
+      // Quick identity check: same byte length AND identical first 200 bytes = same file
+      `must not be identical — FRG-246 bug was that stove_on.png was a copy of stove_off.png ` +
+      `(${result.sampleDiffBytes} byte differences found in first 200 bytes)`
+  // ── TEST 8: Phaser loads both textures as distinct assets in the cache ────────
+  test('Phaser texture cache holds distinct textures for stove_off and stove_on', async ({ page }) => {
+      if (!scene) return { error: 'Phaser scene not accessible' };
+      if (!texOff || texOff.key === '__MISSING') return { error: 'stove_off texture not in Phaser cache' };
+      if (!texOn  || texOn.key  === '__MISSING') return { error: 'stove_on texture not in Phaser cache'  };
+      // In Phaser 3, each texture has a Source with the actual image element.
+      // Identical files loaded under different keys may share or differ in source URL.
+      // Both textures must exist in the cache under their own keys
+        // Width/height: if both were identical 48×48 PNGs they'd match;
+        // distinct assets may differ in dimensions or at least in source URL.
+    // Both textures must be present and have their own distinct keys
+    // Source URLs must differ (each key loads from its own PNG file)
+      'stove_off and stove_on must load from different source files'

Test 3 (Phaser texture cache) fails because Phaser converts image sources to blob URLs when loading them from a static server. The `source[0].image.src` is a blob URL, not the original file path. I need to fix the test assertion to not check for the filename in the source URL.

The good news is that the first 2 tests pass (file sizes are different, content is different). The third test's failure is a test design issue - I used the wrong approach to verify distinctness through Phaser's internal texture source. 

Let me fix the third test to verify that the two textures are distinct in a different way. 

Since Phaser uses blob URLs, the simplest approach is to just verify the blob URLs themselves are different—they'll have unique identifiers since each load generates a fresh blob URL. Alternatively, I could skip the URL check entirely and just confirm the two textures are separate objects, which the different keys already guarantee.

Looking at the test output more carefully, the real evidence that the files differ is in the byte sizes—395 versus 430 bytes—which tests 1 and 2 already captured. The third test was trying to validate this through the Phaser cache, but blob URLs don't give me a reliable source URL to compare. I should rewrite that test to use canvas pixel-level comparison instead. I'm realizing the simplest fix is to just strip out those URL assertions entirely and instead verify the textures exist in Phaser's cache under their correct keys, which the test already does.

Since the fetch calls in tests 1 and 2 already confirm the files are different, I just need test 3 to assert that the blob URLs themselves are different from each other—that's sufficient proof they were loaded as separate assets.

The pixel comparison shows both stove_off and stove_on have the same pixel at position (0,0): `58,58,80,255`. This might be because both images share the same background color for that pixel. The image dimensions are both 48×48 and the first non-transparent pixel happens to be the same.

Looking at the actual files - stove_off.png is 395 bytes and stove_on.png is 430 bytes. They are clearly different files. But the first visible pixel happens to be the same color (which is probably the background/outer edge of the tile).

I need a better pixel sampling strategy - instead of just the first non-transparent pixel, I should compare a hash of more pixels, or compare multiple pixel positions to find a difference.


The background color is likely identical on both sprites since they share the same outer edge, so I need to sample from the center where the burner elements would actually differ. Let me check the center pixel at coordinates (24, 24) instead, which should capture the lit versus unlit state.

# AI Aquarium — QA Test Suite

Playwright regression tests for [AI Aquarium](https://ai-aquarium-phaser.vercel.app).

Tests run against the live deployment and cover all M1 features.

---

## Setup

```bash
npm install
npx playwright install chromium
```

---

## Running Tests

### Full gate (smoke → regression → critical)

```bash
npx playwright test
```

### By tier

```bash
npm run test:smoke       # Fast smoke (8 tests, ~15s)
npm run test:regression  # Full regression suite
npm run test:critical    # Critical loop integrity
```

### Specific spec files

```bash
# M1 debug dashboard
npx playwright test tests/regression/m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js

# M1 activity duration + AI cycle
npx playwright test tests/regression/m1-activity-duration.spec.js

# Slot system / capacity
npx playwright test tests/regression/slot-system.spec.js

# Talk handshaking
npx playwright test tests/regression/talk-handshaking.spec.js

# Fallback logic
npx playwright test tests/regression/fallback-logic.spec.js
```

### With UI (for debugging)

```bash
npx playwright test --ui
```

---

## Configuration

| Setting | Default | Override |
|---------|---------|----------|
| `BASE_URL` | `https://ai-aquarium-phaser.vercel.app` | `.env` file or env var |
| Timeout | 60s per test | `playwright.config.js` |
| Retries | 1 | `playwright.config.js` |

To run against a local or staging deployment:

```bash
BASE_URL=http://localhost:3000 npx playwright test
```

---

## Test Structure

```
tests/
├── helpers.js                          # Shared helpers (waitForEngine, pollEngineState, waitForDashboard)
├── smoke/
│   └── app-loads.spec.js               # Page loads, engine init, 3 characters, 10 objects
├── regression/
│   ├── ai-decisions.spec.js            # AI LLM requests, valid action types, 30s interval, dialogue fields
│   ├── character-movement.spec.js      # A* pathfinding, tile movement, bounds check
│   ├── character-facing.spec.js        # FRG-244: facing toward objects + characters during talk
│   │                                   #   (runs against local dev server at localhost:5173)
│   ├── walk-animation.spec.js          # FRG-204: walk animation stutter/freeze fix
│   │                                   #   moveTo resets walk state, large-delta frame accumulation,
│   │                                   #   full 4-frame cycle, walkTimer remainder invariant
│   │                                   #   (runs against local dev server at localhost:5174)
│   ├── contextual-interaction-animations.spec.js  # FRG-243: contextual anim per object type,
│   │                                   #   spriteKey/interactAnim during+after interaction, live sim,
│   │                                   #   all 9 interaction sprites (runs against localhost:3000)
│   ├── object-interaction.spec.js      # Object state transitions, debug panel
│   ├── speech-bubbles.spec.js          # Dialogue lines, pendingSpeech, speech timer
│   ├── m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js      # Debug page layout, real-time updates, pause/resume,
│   │                                   #   position/facing/target fields, reset behavior, speed controls
│   ├── m1-activity-duration.spec.js    # Activity timers, 30s AI cycle, action types
│   ├── slot-system.spec.js             # Slot/capacity display, pip indicators, reservations,
│   │                                   #   multi-capacity simultaneous use (kotatsu/tv)
│   ├── talk-handshaking.spec.js        # TALK/REJECT events, talking state, canTalk filter
│   └── fallback-logic.spec.js          # FALLBACK events, plan changes, reservation release
└── critical/
    └── sim-loop-integrity.spec.js      # End-to-end: LLM → move → object change → no errors
```

---

## Feature Coverage Map

| M1 Feature | Spec File(s) |
|------------|-------------|
| Characters: Maple, Sol, Nyx | `smoke/app-loads.spec.js`, `m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js` |
| AI decisions every ~30s | `ai-decisions.spec.js`, `m1-activity-duration.spec.js` |
| Action types: move, interact, idle, talk | `ai-decisions.spec.js`, `m1-activity-duration.spec.js` |
| LLM response: dialogue + description fields | `ai-decisions.spec.js` |
| A* pathfinding, bounds | `character-movement.spec.js`, `m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js` |
| Object state machines (idle → active → idle) | `object-interaction.spec.js`, `m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js` |
| Activity hold timers | `m1-activity-duration.spec.js`, `m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js` |
| Smart objects slot system (capacity, pips) | `slot-system.spec.js` |
| Multi-capacity simultaneous use (kotatsu, tv) | `slot-system.spec.js` |
| Walk animation: moveTo reset, large-delta drain, 4-frame cycle | `walk-animation.spec.js` |
| Character facing toward objects on interact | `character-facing.spec.js` |
| Character facing toward each other on talk | `character-facing.spec.js` |
| Contextual interaction animations (reading/sit/phone) | `contextual-interaction-animations.spec.js` |
| Talk handshaking (TALK/REJECT events) | `talk-handshaking.spec.js` |
| Fallback logic (FALLBACK events) | `fallback-logic.spec.js` |
| Debug dashboard: layout + real-time | `m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js` |
| Debug dashboard: position, facing, target fields | `m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js` |
| Speed controls (functional 2x/4x verify) | `m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js` |
| Reset button (behavior, not just visibility) | `m1-debug-dashboard.spec.js` |
| Full sim loop integrity | `critical/sim-loop-integrity.spec.js` |

---

## CI (GitHub Actions)

Tests run automatically on every push and pull request via `.github/workflows/playwright.yml`.

Results are stored as artifacts (JSON report + test-results folder).

To run CI locally with the same config:

```bash
npm ci
npx playwright install --with-deps chromium
npx playwright test
```

---

## Known Issues

| Issue | File | Notes |
|-------|------|-------|
| `talk` action rarely observed in short runs | `talk-handshaking.spec.js` | AI is non-deterministic; tests gracefully skip if not observed |
| REJECT events are probabilistic | `talk-handshaking.spec.js` | Only fires when two characters attempt simultaneous talk |
| FALLBACK events require 30s+ observation | `fallback-logic.spec.js` | Events appear after AI cycle + object contention |
| Sim clock ratio ~36x in headless (vs 60x in browser) | `critical/sim-loop-integrity.spec.js` | RAF throttled in headless — documented, not a product bug |

---

input.content:
# AI Aquarium QA — Project Memory

## Project Overview
Playwright regression test suite for AI Aquarium (Phaser 3 simulation game).
- Test repo: `/home/gem/project` (QA tests)
- Source repo: cloned on-demand from `$SOURCE_REPO` env var, usually from `.env`
- Tests run against local dev servers at hardcoded ports per feature branch

## Test Structure
- `tests/smoke/` — fast smoke (8 tests)
- `tests/regression/` — feature regression suites
- `tests/critical/` — end-to-end sim loop
- `tests/helpers.js` — shared helpers

## Source Repo Architecture (master)
- **Characters**: `Character.js` — `name` ('Amelia','Lucy','Ash'), `label` ('Maple','Sol','Nyx')
  - NO `.id` property (old tests used `.id` but master uses `.name`)
  - Methods: `moveTo()`, `startTalk()`, `startInteract()`, `_playDirectionalIdle()`, `_updateFacingFromNextTile()`
- **Engine**: `Engine.js` — `_startConversation(charA, charB)`, `objectEngine.getById(id)`
- **Scene**: `scene.js` — `window._gameEngine = this.engine` set unconditionally in `create()`
  - Access Phaser scene: `engine.characters[0].sprite.scene`
  - `WALK_DIRS` defines animation frame ranges (up=48-53, down=288-293, right/left=144-149)
  - `IDLE_DIRS` defines idle frame ranges (down=0-5, left=6-11, right=12-17, up=18-23)
- **Objects**: `objects.js` — `getById(id)`, `getAll()`, objects have `spriteMap`, `state`

## Dev Server Setup
- Source repo has NO `package.json` — serve with `python3 -m http.server <PORT> &`
- Wait for server: `curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" http://localhost:<PORT>/`
- `window._gameEngine` already exposed in master's `scene.js` — no injection needed
- For `window._gameScene`: inject via `page.route('**/scene.js*')` replacing the engine line

## Key Injection Patterns
```javascript
// Inject _gameScene (for Phaser animation checks)
await page.route('**/scene.js*', async (route) => {
  const response = await route.fetch();
  let body = await response.text();
  body = body.replace(
    'window._gameEngine = this.engine;',
    'window._gameEngine = this.engine;\n    window._gameScene = this;'
  );
  await route.fulfill({ response, body });
});

// Access Phaser scene without injection (simpler)
const scene = engine.characters[0].sprite.scene;
const anim = scene.anims.get('Amelia_walk_up');
```

## Port History
- 5173: character-facing (old feature branch)
- 5174: walk-animation (old feature branch)
- 5176: object-sprite-swap (old feature branch)
- 5177: master branch (2026-03-13)

## Phaser Gotchas
- Animation frames: `anim.frames.map(f => parseInt(f.frame.name, 10))` to get frame numbers
- Texture source URLs are blob URLs in headless Chrome — can't match filename in URL
- Compare textures via pixel-sum: draw to canvas, sum all RGB values
- Idle animations: 4 directional × 6 frames = 24 total frames per idle spritesheet
- Walk animations: 6 frames per direction from walk spritesheet (2304×864, 48×96 each)

## Bug History
- FRG-204 (timer): walk frame timer while-loop fix (localhost:5174)
- FRG-204 (sprite-row): walk_up changed from frames 0-3 (row 0) to 48-53 (row 1)
- FRG-244 (facing): characters face objects/each other during talk
- FRG-244 (directional idle): startTalk plays {name}_idle_{facing} not {name}_idle_anim
- FRG-243: contextual interaction animations (reading/sit/phone)
- FRG-245: collision avoidance
- FRG-246: stove_on.png asset was identical to stove_off.png — replaced with distinct lit variant
  - stove_off.png = 395 bytes, stove_on.png = 430 bytes (master, 2026-03-13)
  - Pixel sum: off=508581, on=567861

## Test Results Summary (2026-03-13)
Total: 111 tests passing (102 original + 9 new for FRG-204/244/246 visual fixes)
